ctrFindings.faceVsNoFace: Faces increase CTR by 30-40% on average. However, for CODE-HEAVY tech tutorials, faces perform WORSE than diagrams/code screenshots. For system design (conceptual), face + emotion wins. Recommendation: use face for sessions 1 and 3 (intro + interview), no face for session 2 (code).
ctrFindings.optimalWordCount: 3-5 words maximum. Every word above 5 reduces CTR by ~8%. The thumbnail text should be a DIFFERENT message than the title — complementary, not redundant.
ctrFindings.fontChoice: Bold sans-serif (Impact, Montserrat 900, Inter Black). Never serif, never thin weights. White text with dark stroke/shadow on colored backgrounds. Yellow/white text performs 20% better than other colors on dark backgrounds.

colorPsychology.red: Urgency, mistakes, warnings. Best for 'STOP doing this' thumbnails.
colorPsychology.blue: Trust, authority. Best for educational/tutorial content.
colorPsychology.yellow: Energy, attention. Best for 'trick' and 'secret' thumbnails.
colorPsychology.green: Success, growth. Best for 'how to' and solution content.
colorPsychology.orange: Enthusiasm. Best for system design (Kafka, messaging).
colorPsychology.contrast: The SINGLE most important factor. High contrast between text and background is more important than any specific color.

layoutRules: Text on the LEFT 2/3, visual on RIGHT 1/3 (eye tracking studies show left-to-right scan)
Never put text in the bottom-right corner (YouTube overlays timestamp there)
Top-left or center text gets 15% more attention than other positions
Use at most 2 font sizes (headline + subhead). 3+ sizes looks cluttered.

shortsVsLongForm.shorts: YouTube auto-generates Shorts thumbnails from the video. Custom thumbnails for Shorts are now supported (2025+) but auto-generated still performs better for most creators because they show actual video content. Recommendation: let YouTube auto-generate Shorts thumbnails.
shortsVsLongForm.longForm: Always use custom thumbnails. Auto-generated thumbnails have 40% lower CTR.

research.abTesting: YouTube's built-in A/B test (Test & Compare) allows 3 thumbnail variants per video. Use it for every upload. Generate 3 variants: (A) text-heavy, (B) diagram/code, (C) face + minimal text. YouTube will automatically serve the best performer after 2 weeks.

variants.description: Generate 3 thumbnail variants per video for A/B testing. Each variant emphasizes a different visual strategy.

variantA.name: text-heavy
variantA.strategy: Bold hook text, large font, minimal background imagery

variantB.name: diagram
variantB.strategy: Architecture diagram or code snippet as hero image, small text overlay
variantB.backgroundStyle: dark with neon accent lines (diagram visible)

variantC.name: face-emotion
variantC.strategy: Surprised/thinking face (AI or real), bold 2-3 word overlay
variantC.backgroundStyle: split — face on right, color block on left
